title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Contract Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective authentication and credential lifecycle policy; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authentication and credential lifecycle policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
